brief_justification: Arrives at the exact correct answer (No) with fully valid reasoning showing the 34ms difference is unaffected by ~0.013ms combined uncertainty; traces second/third-order effects including the 66ms margin to the threshold and the principle that uncertainty only matters near the boundary; proceeds linearly without naming biases, checking assumptions, or reflecting on its method; applies explicit worst-case boundary condition testing and derives the full possible range of true differences; presents the conclusion with reasonable grounding but does not flag assumptions such as error independence or absence of other timing factors; considers only the two stated uncertainty sources with no evaluation of alternatives (e.g., network latency, other systematic biases, or different interpretations of the question); every step from raw timestamps through uncertainty magnitudes to final range is fully shown and reproducible.
